id.description: Stable content hash of (channel_name + contributor + summary). Used for dedupe and to link triage decisions to the original record. Identical across an item's decision history.

timestamp.description: UTC ISO-8601 timestamp this line was written into the queue.

contributor.description: Display name / handle of the sender. Stored separately from the body.

channel_name.description: Allowlisted chat / channel / mailbox the message came from.

source_message_id.description: Native message id on the source platform (Telegram message_id, email Message-ID, WhatsApp messageId). Audit field; not used for dedupe.

summary.description: Short one-line summary derived from the raw message.

kind.description: Fine-grained type. Preserves the complaint vs suggestion distinction.

category.description: Coarse triage bucket. Defaults to needs_review if unclear.

evidence.description: URLs / file refs / screenshots mentioned in the message.

raw_message.description: Verbatim message body as received. Never edited; kept for audit.

junk_reason.description: Set when status is junk. Deterministic rule that flagged the message (e.g. link_only, too_short, spam_phrase:...).

decision_reason.description: Human reason recorded by the triage CLI when a decision is appended.

decided_at.description: UTC ISO-8601 timestamp of the triage decision.

thread_ref.description: Optional thread/conversation reference (chat id, email References).

contact_ref.description: Optional contact reference for follow-up (handle / address). Minimise PII.
